zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London於1062年12月3日出生在英國奧可福爾利可特時，他的父親Geoffrey de Mandeville27歲，母親Adeliza Balts23歲。他於大約1095年與Margaret Rie在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1130年1月6日在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 沃爾坦享年67歲，埋葬在法國豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利莫莫。

nameMeanings.name: Mandeville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Gabrielle, Camille, Emile, Fernand, Fernande, Gaston, Jean-Paul, Lucien, Michel.

French: habitational name from Mandeville, the name of two places in Normandy, derived from Latin magna villa ‘large estate’, or a variant of Manneville, a habitational name from Manneville, the name of several places in Normandy (see 2 below). Compare Montville and Monville .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Manneville (Seine-Maritime, Calvados), Manneville-sur-Risle (Eure), or Magneville (Manche).

variants.name: Mansfield
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire. The placename means ‘open land by the river Maun’, from the river name Maun (which may take its name from a hill named with Old English mamme ‘teat’) + Old English feld ‘open country’.
Irish (Waterford): when not an importation of 1, this is an altered form of the Norman name de Manville (see Mandeville 3).
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Mansfeld, a habitational name from a place so called in Saxony.

variants.name: Manderville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manwell
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manville
meanings: English (Sussex and Lancashire): variant of   Mandeville  .
Altered form of   Mainville   1, a surname of Swiss French origin (see   Miville  ).
French: variant of Menville, a habitational name from Menville in Haute-Garonne, and also of Mainville 2.

variants.name: Mundell
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .
Americanized form of German Mündel (or some other similar, like-sounding surname): nickname for someone with a prominent mouth, from Middle High German munt, mündelīn ‘(little) mouth’.

variants.name: Monville
meanings: Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
Walloon: habitational name from Monville in Namur province in Wallonia (Belgium), of the same etymology as in 3 below.
In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Montville in Seine-Maritime or Monville in Calvados, both named with Old French mont ‘mountain, hill’ + ville ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Mandal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Chandi, Sanjay, Sankar, Somnath, Aditi, Aditya, Ajit, Amala, Amitabha, Anil, Anirudh, Aparna.
Indian (northern states): from Sanskrit maṇḍala, a word that has several meanings, including ‘sphere’ and ‘halo’.
English (Cumbria; of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Montville
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Vernice.
Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
French: variant of   Monville  . This surname is very rare in France, found mainly in Creuse.

variants.name: Manfull
meanings: English (Midlands): probably a variant of   Mandeville  . Compare   Manville  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London was born on 3 December 1062, in Rycote, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Geoffrey de Mandeville, was 27 and his mother, Adeliza Balts, was 23. He married Margaret Rie about 1095, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 6 January 1130, in Great Waltham, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 67, and was buried in Mortemer, Seine-Maritime, Upper Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London est né le 3 décembre 1062, à Rycote, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Geoffrey de Mandeville, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Adeliza Balts, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Rie environ 1095, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 6 janvier 1130, à Great Waltham, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Mortemer, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London am 3. Dezember 1062 in Rycote, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Geoffrey de Mandeville 27 und seine Mutter Adeliza Balts 23. Er hat um 1095 in England Margaret Rie geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 6. Januar 1130 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Great Waltham, Essex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Mortemer, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in Londonが1062年12月3日にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアリコトで生まれた時，父Geoffrey de Mandevilleは27歳，母Adeliza Baltsは23歳であった。約1095年にイギリスでMargaret Rieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1130年1月6日にイギリスイギリスエセックスグレアト ワルサムで67歳で死去し，フランスハウテ-ノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメモーテマーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London(이)가 1062년 12월 3일 영국 스퍼드실르 리컷에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey de Mandeville(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Adeliza Balts(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1095년 영국에서 Margaret Rie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1130년 1월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 월섬에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 하테-노먼디 세이인-마이팀 모르테머에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London nasceu em 3 de dezembro de 1062, em Rycote, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Geoffrey de Mandeville, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Adeliza Balts, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Rie aproximadamente 1095, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 6 de janeiro de 1130, em Great Waltham, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Mortemer, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Mandeville Constable at the Tower in London родился 3 декабря 1062 г. в местности рикот, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Geoffrey de Mandeville было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Adeliza Balts, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Rie около 1095 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 6 января 1130 г. в местности грит валтам, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности мортемир, сине-маритим, хауте-норманди, Франция.
